subject: An update on President Trump’s fight for border security
body:
No more political games. It’s time to come together and make a deal. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aa1d12260f&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • January 10, 2019
---
| | | **Millions of Americans** , both Republican and Democrat, voted for President Donald J. Trump to reverse decades of failure by Washington and fix America’s broken immigration system.

**With that election,** the American people sent a message that they are tired of politicians talking one way about our border and then acting another.

**President Trump will keep his promise.** He has offered a strong, common-sense proposal to stop gang members, drug smugglers, and human traffickers from exploiting our Southern border. It’s a goal most Democrats claim to share. But when the Administration requested $5.7 billion—about .01 percent of the 2018 Federal budget—for construction of a border wall, Democrats walked away.

**No more political games.** It’s time to come together and make a deal.
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2023dd586e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
Watch: “CBP and DHS are committed to both our border security mission, as well as our humanitarian mission. Mr. President, Senators, we couldn't do it without your support."
|
---
|
## **Democrats _say_ they want border security**** **

**This week,** Democratic leaders exposed their indifference and denial when they called the humanitarian and security crisis at our border “a manufactured crisis.” It turns out many Democrats, [including President Obama](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8a1fb0fe5&e=3ee4985eea), don’t agree.

**The stories of suffering are real,** as President Trump told Americans on Tuesday night. Each week, 300 of our citizens are killed by heroin alone—90 percent of which floods across our Southern border. The gaps in our security leave a pathway for drug cartels and human traffickers, who prey on vulnerable migrants and their children.

**Open borders empower criminals, too**. In California, an Air Force veteran was raped and brutally murdered by an illegal alien with a long criminal history. In Maryland, MS-13 gang members who arrived in our country as unaccompanied minors were charged with viciously stabbing a 16-year-old girl last year.

**These aren’t isolated crimes.** In the last two years, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers have arrested more than a quarter-million aliens with criminal records—including those charged with or convicted of 100,000 assaults, 30,000 sex crimes, and 4,000 murders.

**To their credit,** rank-and-file Democrats have started breaking with their leaders, admitting that physical barriers are needed. “I don’t understand [Speaker Nancy Pelosi] saying it’s ‘immoral,’” Sen. Angus King (I-ME) says. “Certainly you need barriers and we support barriers,” Sen. Ben Cardin (D-MD) explains.

**Your voice matters.** Washington is starting to listen. “If I am getting comments and contact from my constituents expressing concern that the Democrats are not prioritizing security, then I think we can do better,” Rep. Abigail Spanberger (D-Va.) says.

[In case you missed it: President Trump addresses the Nation.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7481eaffa0&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Setting the record straight**** **

**With the mainstream media** often acting like Democratic surrogates instead of objective watchdogs, a lot of misinformation is spreading about border security and the current Government shutdown. Senior Administration officials have been working hard to correct the record:
* Criminals and terrorists can and do exploit our porous border. “The threat is real,” Homeland Security Secretary Kirstjen Nielsen says—the number of individuals on [the terror watchlist encountered](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ec0f826892&e=3ee4985eea) at our Southern border has increased over the last two years.

* Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-CA) says using a physical barrier to protect our border is “immoral.” [Press Secretary Sarah Sanders](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4e1cdb74cd&e=3ee4985eea) is pushing for clarity: “So does Pelosi want to tear down the hundreds of miles of barrier that already exist and many Democrats voted for?”

* Most important, [the crisis on the border is real](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0fda942107&e=3ee4985eea). Four-in-five [Americans agree](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=158cb8377a&e=3ee4985eea).

---
|
---
|
## **4 things the West Wing is reading today**** **

**1.** “There is a humanitarian and security crisis at our southwest border. It is getting worse, and a “status quo” response from Congress — inadequate authorities, insufficient funds and indifference to the chaos — will not cut it.”

[Read more: Homeland Security Secretary Nielsen in _USA Today_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=43807c2c4a&e=3ee4985eea)

**2.** Mark Morgan, a Border Patrol chief under former President Obama, “said he’s most frustrated by how children are brought into the country illegally under perilous conditions because coyotes exploit vulnerabilities at the border . . . ‘I don’t understand why anyone would be against developing a process that stops that from happening.’”

[Read more: Obama Border Patrol chief: ‘Stay the course’ on the border wall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dd95c482ee&e=3ee4985eea)

**3.** Politicians “are not the ones that are investigating the crimes. They are not the ones out here when it’s 120 degrees, processing a crime scene where 14 people were left to die in the desert,” says Leon Wilmot, Sheriff in Yuma County, Arizona.

[Read more: Sheriff: Border Fence Helped Cut Crime in Yuma by 91 Percent](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b44b97b507&e=3ee4985eea)

**4.** “Most important, Pelosi and Schumer failed to use the one word that millions of Americans were longing to hear — compromise. But Trump did. That is why the president won the night,” Marc Thiessen of the American Enterprise Institute writes.

[Read more: Trump won the night. Schumer and Pelosi lost.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=08dd3bd5de&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|

Friend,

When President Trump accepted the Republican nomination in 2016 he promised he would always speak for YOU, the forgotten American.
_" These are the forgotten men and women of our country. And they are forgotten, but they're not going to be forgotten long. These are people who work hard but no longer have a voice. **I am your voice.** "_
Tuesday night, President Trump stood firm in front of the entire nation and made good on that promise once again.

He clearly laid out the issues at our Southern Border, pointed out that Democrats have wanted a wall in the past, and DEMANDED Democrats quit playing games and protect American citizens.

We were so thankful to him for speaking out for all Americans. As one of our top supporters, we wanted to give you a special opportunity to say thank you.

body:
http://lipinskiforcongress.com [http://lipinskiforcongress.com]
Hi Peter,
I wanted to thank all of you for taking the time to participate in our survey this week. We received hundreds and hundreds of responses, and an outpouring of support for mine and my colleagues’ efforts to fix Congress.
More than half of you said that you were most excited about our proposal to commit the House to a more fair and inclusive process — one where more ideas and amendments are debated and there is less of a reliance on closed rules.
Thank you!
I’ll be pushing hard to make sure my colleagues — on both sides of the aisle — respect this and the rest of our proposals, but knowing my supporters have my back will make that much easier.
Be sure to like my Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/danlipinskiforcongress/] and follow my Twitter [https://twitter.com/dlipinski03?lang=en] to stay up to date on the legislation we’re working on in the 116th Congress.
Like on Facebook [https://www.facebook.com/danlipinskiforcongress/] Follow on Twitter [https://twitter.com/dlipinski03?lang=en]
Thanks,
Dan

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: About this shutdown…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
Peter, My first days in Congress have been a mix of renewed hope and great frustration. On the one hand, I am surrounded by a new group of young, diverse, service-driven leaders who are ready to make government work for the people again. We're eager to get big money out of our politics, bring down the cost of health care, strengthen our public schools, and so much more.
| | Peter,

My first days in Congress have been a mix of renewed hope and great frustration.

On the one hand, I am surrounded by a new group of young, diverse, service-driven leaders who are ready to make government work for the people again. We're eager to get big money out of our politics, bring down the cost of health care, strengthen our public schools, and so much more.

**But there 's one big problem: We're in day 21 of a government shutdown.**

This is exactly the kind of partisan brinkmanship that people across this country are fed up with. It's shameful that hundreds of thousands of federal workers are going without pay, and that our country's stability and security hang in the balance. That's why, on Day One in Congress, I took action to reopen the government, and it's why I won't accept any pay until the government reopens and federal workers get paid, too.

[**To hear more of my thoughts on the current shutdown, click here to watch my interview on _MSNBC_.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/vzgapU9h_NbHZwP3kRSGk8WaaGPZyAYaICKtPnpGxG4=/oJDWvAZQSWnqW1Djy0pfveQ55XfV5dkYb6Z4aHD0s_o=)

Voters across Central Virginia, as well as everyone who is a part of this incredible grassroots network, put their trust in me to start fixing what is broken in Washington. We have a challenging road ahead, but I remain committed to working day in, day out to serve our community and demonstrate that trust was well warranted.

I'll be sure to keep you informed of our efforts and progress.

Sincerely,

Abigail _Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/vzgapU9h_NbHZwP3kRSGk8WaaGPZyAYaICKtPnpGxG4=/-RNoTzw2nsJQMZ-cusW2pinopFhfZPwyLQ1IlHHUx_U=)_

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Sign my petition to reopen the government →
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek:

Today marks 21 days of a government shutdown--tied for the _longest shutdown in our country's history_. It goes without saying, but I'm gonna say it anyway: **This is completely ridiculous.**

Right now, President Trump is manufacturing a crisis and punishing millions of Americans--including 800,000 federal workers who are going without paychecks--all out of a petulant desire get his way and back up an idiotic promise that Mexico would pay for a border wall. Enough is enough!

[Add your name to my petition right now to tell President Trump and Mitch McConnell to reopen the government.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175a/3e0906/7a1d3142/3e375ff4/621769596/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/reopen-the-government?source=20190111_1_PET_TK_Shutdown&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+Add+your+name+to+my+petition+right+now+t&utm_campaign=20190111_1_PET_TK_Shutdown")
I've heard from federal workers all across Virginia who are worried about their mortgages. They're worried about paying their heating bills. They're worried about affording prescriptions for their kids. And just because the President hasn't gotten his way--and Mitch McConnell won't stand up to him--those worries are only compounding for these workers and their families.

Derek, it's not just our 800,000 federal workers who are impacted by this shutdown. It's hurting millions of American citizens. Take a minute and think about all these folks:
* People receiving domestic violence services
* Families applying for food stamps
* Small businesses who can't apply for SBA loans
* Farmers who need advice from extension agents
* Families who can't take their kids to national parks and forests

**Let's send a message to President Trump: We stand with federal workers and all who are affected by this senseless shutdown. It 's embarrassing our country, and we want it to end. And while we're at it, we can send a message to Mitch McConnell, too. If he called a vote right now, the Senate would overwhelmingly vote to reopen the government. So why won't he do it?**

[President Trump and Mitch McConnell: We have got to reopen the government and quit punishing 800,000 federal workers--NOW.](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f175a/3e0906/7a1d3142/3e375fcb/621769596/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/reopen-the-government?source=20190111_1_PET_TK_Shutdown&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+President+Trump+and+Mitch+McConnell+We+h&utm_campaign=20190111_1_PET_TK_Shutdown")

Thanks for standing up with me and with government workers all around Virginia and America who deserve better.

_Tim_

| **_Secure Our Border Now_** On Tuesday, I hope you had a chance to watch **President Donald Trump's** address to the nation from the Oval Office. He spoke directly to the American people about the urgent need to address the security and humanitarian crisis on our southern border and to secure the funding we need for critical border security.

In case you missed it, you can watch his [full address here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6431cf4b8b&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Here in Indiana, we understand the need for securing our border. As our leaders are working toward solutions on the opioid crisis, for instance, thousands of pounds of illegal drugs entered the country through our southern border last year. We may be miles away from the border, but this crisis affects us all.

And that's one reason why Indiana's Republican Congressional delegation is standing firmly behind President Trump right now on the need to fund border security.

Here's what our _TWO_ Republican U.S. Senators are saying:

_"The president made it clear why border security is so important. But Democrats are refusing to address the humanitarian and security crisis at our southern border."_
**-Senator Todd Young **

_"President Trump has made a courageous decision to secure the border and to stop illegal immigration, meanwhile Democrats have refused to address this crisis or offer any solutions."_
**-Senator Mike Braun**

It's clear that we must secure our southern border. It's also clear that Hoosier Republicans in Congress are working to find a solution. What's unclear, though, is whether Democrats are willing to do what it takes to keep our country safe.

-Kyle Hupfer

P.S. Congratulations to the new chairman of the Marion County Republican Party, Bryce Carpenter. It's going to be a big year, and we know Chairman Carpenter's leadership & the work of county chairs across the state will help lead us to more victories in 2019 and beyond!

_**PHOTO:** Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer recently met President Trump in the Oval Office -- the site of Tuesday's prime time address to the nation. _

| **_Let the 2019 Legislative Session...Begin!_** This week marked the first full week of the 2019 legislative session. While bills are being filed and committees are starting to meet, **Governor Eric Holcomb** is already moving full steam ahead to deliver Next Level results for Hoosiers! That includes [introducing his budget proposal](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0d6e464e9d&e=1acd9bb3d6), which would be Indiana's _EIGHTH_ consecutive balanced budget! It keeps our state pensions funded and our debt low (talk about good government!).

He also spoke at the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Indiana Holiday Celebration at the Statehouse, continuing the legacy of the late civil rights leader.

And next Tuesday, he'll hit the airwaves statewide to deliver his third State of the State Address at 7 p.m. ET. **Governor Holcomb** will talk about the momentum we've built together throughout the state, and the work ahead of us to continue taking Indiana to the Next Level. Make sure to mark your calendars, because this is a speech you won't want to miss!
